name: thread6

metadata.name: thread6
metadata.summary: A plug n play multithreading interface

documentation:
# thread6
Simple parallel processing interface for python

## Why?
Python's built in parallel processing and threading library is pretty simple to implement but sometimes you just want to chuck data at a function and make it run faster

## Requirements
Python 3+

## Installation

## Quickstart
Use the `threaded` decorator to turn a method into a threaded method. That's it!
```python
@thread6.threaded()
def threaded_print():
    print("")
    return 1
```

Alternatively, use `run_threaded` function
```python
thread6.run_threaded(threaded_print)
```

Both the `threaded` decorator and `run_threaded` method will return an instance of
`ResultThread`. This allow you to optionally wait for the function to finish executing 
and get the return value. To get the return value, use `.await_output()`
```python
result = threaded_print()
result.await_output()  # this will return 1
```

If you have a function that needs to execute on a large list of data, use `run_chunked`
```python
def update_items(items):
    ...

items = [...]
thread6.run_chunked(update_items, items)
```
`.await_output()` also work with `run_chunked` but will return a list of return values instead

## Usage

functions.name: thread6.thread6.threaded
documentation:
A decorator to run a function in a separate thread, this is useful
when you want to do any IO operations (network request, prints, etc...)
and want to do something else while waiting for it to finish.

```
param fx

the function to run in a separate thread

param daemon

boolean whether or not to run as a daemon thread

return

whatever fx returns
```

functions.name: thread6.thread6.run_threaded
documentation:
Helper function to run a function in a separate thread

```
param fx

the function to run in a separate thread

param args

list arguments to pass to fx

param kwargs

dictionary keyword arguments to pass to fx

return

whatever fx returns
```

functions.name: thread6.thread6.run_chunked
documentation:
Given an list of data, split it into smaller chunks
and feed it to the processing function(fx) to be runned
in separate threads. fx must take arguments in the format:

fx(list, args, kwargs)

```
param fx

the processing function to run

param dataset

list to split

param threads

int how many threads to use when splitting
the dataset and running the function

param args

list of arguments to pass to fx

param kwargs

dictionary of keyword arguments to pass to fx

return

an instance of _pp.MultiThreadManager
```

classes.name: thread6.thread6.ResultThread

methods.name: thread6.thread6.ResultThread.await_output
documentation: Wait for the thread to finish and return the return value of fx

A custom Thread that can return the value of the function 
runned inside it

classes.name: thread6.thread6.MultiThreadManager

documentation:
Initialise a MultiThreadManager

```
param threads

a list of ResultThread objects

return

None
```

methods.name: thread6.thread6.MultiThreadManager.add_thread
documentation:
Add a thread for this manager to manage

```
param thread

a ResultThread object

return

None
```

methods.name: thread6.thread6.MultiThreadManager.get_threads
documentation:
Return a list of threads this manager is managing

```
return

list of ResultThread objects
```

methods.name: thread6.thread6.MultiThreadManager.start_all
documentation:
Attempt to start all threads. This will ignore the RunTimeError
raised when Thread.start() is called multiple time

methods.name: thread6.thread6.MultiThreadManager.await_output
documentation:
Wait for all threads to finish executing and return a list
of all return values

```
return

list of objects
```

A manager to manage the execution of multiple threads. Provide
method to return a list of return values
